qa.overall_assessment: ITER-2 VERDICT. BA iter-1 GENUINELY IMPROVED 8 of 10 prior objections — substantial improvement. Adversarial substitutability attacks could not break AC-01, AC-02, AC-03, AC-04, AC-05, AC-10, AC-11, AC-13. However, BA iter-1 introduced 2 NEW critical defects: AC-07 SHA-equality check (per_path_survival_and_content_match) fails for mutable runtime telemetry that already drifts from HEAD BEFORE dev acts (cp-state-qa.json and agent-scores.json) — would unfairly fail compliant dev work; AC-09 specs_exclusion_runtime fixture has a hard ImportError (relative import from .extract) AND tests a vacuous condition (tree.py already filters dot-prefixed names so .claude is excluded today regardless of SKIP_FILES augmentation) — pass-as-substitute scenario. Both confirmed by independent codex review. Verdict FAIL. BA iter-3 must address ONLY AC-07 and AC-09 — do NOT re-touch the 8 resolved ACs. Out-of-scope observation re: git rm --cached is acceptable as BA-DERIVED_WORK scope-labeled cleanup; the scope label and out_of_scope_observation routing is correctly threaded for orchestrator awareness.

OBJ-01.verification: allowed_tools_must_include now lists all 8 tools; denied_write_path_prefixes_must_include lists all 10 paths. Dev cannot pass with 3-tool list or subset of denial prefixes.

OBJ-02.verification: Third site at commands/dev.md:134-136 added via shell_token_check. scope_start_regex verified to match line 133 exactly; scope_end_regex alternation matches line 136. comment_strip + shell_token semantics forbid not-test-writer substring + comment bypass.

OBJ-03.verification: Paragraph-scoped per block. step-5 scope (line 183-184) regex captures one-line paragraph; backtick-aware must_not_contain regex matches actual offending text with negative lookahead for annotation. Output JSON Report scope (line 189-213) requires per-task manifest_path AND kind=index reference; current line 198 fails must_not_contain -> dev forced to change.

OBJ-04.verification: Phase 5 scope (line 996-1006) requires per-task path, per-task pytest, sentinel preservation via verbatim phrase. Schema scope (line 1433-1442) requires per-task manifest_path. Current state fails must_contain or must_not_contain in multiple places -> dev forced to update.

OBJ-05.verification: Step 11 scope (line 772-818) and Dev-handoff scope (line 666-667 anchors) both require per-task path appearance and forbid unannotated global. Test-writer dispatch input at line 633 correctly OUT OF SCOPE.

OBJ-06.verification: Scope label DERIVED_WORK correctly added; ls-files-empty check is sound. However, the per-path SHA equality check has a hard defect — see OBJ-11.

OBJ-07.verification: ast.literal_eval set membership check is sound. However, the inline runtime fixture script has a hard ImportError AND tests a vacuous condition — see OBJ-12.

OBJ-08.verification: Shell-aware preprocess (strip_shell_comments + mask_quoted_strings) + per-invocation precedence regex verified manually against actual line forms. Regex accepts subshell-wrap forms and rejects bare source-without-paren and bare python3 calls. Count-substitutability attack eliminated.

OBJ-09.verification: File-wide Sub-step 12.X forbid catches all 4 current hits. Scoped positive checks for spec section updates phrase (Step 12 scope) and score-update events Step 12 (Mascot scope, case-sensitive Step prevents lowercase sub-step trivial match).

OBJ-10.verification: Paragraph-scope anchored to Cycle-2 user-facing anchor — existing line 320 paragraph cannot satisfy. Four required items (spec ref + rank label + AskUserQuestion + BOTH-sides clause) all paragraph-scoped. BOTH-sides regex verified to NOT trivially match existing text.

objections.finding: AC-07.per_path_survival_and_content_match script enforces git hash-object P equals git rev-parse HEAD:P for every prior-HEAD path. Verified live in current working tree (BEFORE any dev work): TWO files already drift from HEAD — .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-qa.json (HEAD fd540c... vs WT fa4ddf...) and agent-scores.json (HEAD 41c522... vs WT 14b30c...). Both are inherently mutable runtime telemetry: every spec-check.py invocation mutates cp-state files; score-update.sh + score-inject.sh mutate agent-scores.json. The check would FAIL dev's compliant git rm --cached work through no fault of dev's. Dev cannot reset these to HEAD without destroying live runtime state (current cycle's spec-check.py is actively writing cp-state-qa.json). This violates the QA principle 'no unfair fail after correct work'. Codex independently confirmed this finding with verbatim SHA evidence.
objections.evidence: Live execution at 2026-05-20T23:50Z: while IFS read p; do head_sha=git rev-parse HEAD:p; wt_sha=git hash-object p; mismatch echo DRIFT; done over git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only --paths produces 2 DRIFT lines. acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:274-290 contains the SHA-equality script.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace AC-07.per_path_survival_and_content_match with existence-only check: for each path P enumerated by git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only, assert [ -f P ]. Drop the SHA byte-equality assertion entirely — git rm --cached semantics already guarantee working-tree preservation by definition. If BA wants stronger safety against accidental working-tree truncation, capture a pre-dev baseline of file sizes/SHAs at task-id start (e.g., write to .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260520-221452/pre-dev-baseline.txt as part of dev-handoff) and compare working-tree against THAT baseline, not against HEAD. Also: the ticket prose at line 109 says git rm -r --cached but AC-07 line 247-258 only uses git rm --cached (no -r). For directory target .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/, -r is required. Reconcile prose vs check.

objections.finding: AC-09.specs_exclusion_runtime inline Python script has TWO compounding defects: (1) HARD IMPORT ERROR: the script uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(tree, hooks/doc_sync/tree.py) + spec.loader.exec_module(tree_mod) — but hooks/doc_sync/tree.py:5 contains 'from .extract import extract_description' (a relative import). Loading tree.py outside its package context produces ImportError: attempted relative import with no known parent package. I verified this experimentally — the inline script fails before any test is run, regardless of dev's SKIP_FILES augmentation. (2) VACUOUS TEST: even if the import worked, the fixture creates .claude/specs/spec-foo/cp-state.json and calls tree_mod.build_tree(root). But tree.py:22-27 already filters not i.name.startswith('.') — so .claude (dot-prefixed) is filtered today, BEFORE any dev SKIP_FILES change. The runtime test would pass vacuously even if dev forgot to augment SKIP_FILES with .claude/specs/. This is a textbook pass-as-substitute scenario — the check passes whether dev does the work or not. Codex independently confirmed both defects.
objections.evidence: (1) Live execution at 2026-05-20T23:50Z reproduces ImportError verbatim. (2) Verified: package-aware import + build_tree on fixture containing .claude/specs/spec-foo/cp-state.json produces output containing only normal.txt — .claude already excluded by dot-prefix filter. acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:360-374 contains the broken runtime fixture.
objections.proposed_fix: (a) Fix import: PYTHONPATH=PWD python3 -c 'from hooks.doc_sync import tree as tree_mod; ...' so package-relative import works. (b) Make the fixture EXERCISE the actual leak path: call build_tree(root / '.claude') (or simulate the .claude/INDEX.md regeneration) — NOT build_tree(root). Inside .claude, the specs/ subdirectory is NOT dot-prefixed, so the current code does NOT exclude it. After dev augments SKIP_FILES with a .claude/specs predicate, the fixture would prove the exclusion happens. (c) Alternatively or additionally: add a structural check that tree.py has a path-aware .claude/specs skip predicate (e.g., new SKIP_PATH_PREFIXES set containing .claude/specs or equivalent). This catches the leak structurally, not just via fixture probability.

objections._doc: RESOLVED in iter-1. Preserved for audit trail.
objections.finding: AC-01 JSON check at acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:26-30 requires allowed_tools_must_include = [Read, Bash, Skill] — only 3 tools. But ticket-20260520-221452.md:99 explicitly requires the test-writer role to have [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Skill] — 8 tools. Without Write and Edit and MultiEdit in the policy, test-writer literally cannot write or update test files. Dev could pass AC-01 with a 3-tool list and still leave test-writer unable to perform its core function.
objections.proposed_fix: Update AC-01 check.allowed_tools_must_include to list all 8 tools: ['Read', 'Glob', 'Grep', 'Bash', 'Write', 'Edit', 'MultiEdit', 'Skill']. Also add denied_write_path_prefixes assertion to include the full list from ticket (/src/, /app/, /lib/, /.claude/hooks/, /.claude/agents/, /.claude/commands/, /.claude/policies/, /.claude/schemas/, /.claude/scripts/, /.claude/settings.json).
objections.iter1_resolution: RESOLVED — JSON now lists all 8 tools and 10 deny-prefixes.

objections._doc: RESOLVED in iter-1. Preserved for audit trail.
objections.iter1_resolution: RESOLVED — AST-set membership + shell-token check now covers all 3 sites including commands/dev.md:134-136.
objections.finding: AC-02 verifies test-writer presence in CP_AGENTS (hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py) and agent_types (hooks/prompt-workflow.py) — 2 sites. BA missed a THIRD site: commands/dev.md:134-136 has a manual-fallback agent enumeration loop (`for agent in architect ba cleaner ... ui-specialist user`) that does not include test-writer. If the hook auto-init is bypassed (session resumed), this loop runs and STILL fails to create the test-writer registry sentinel. Codex finding #2 confirmed by direct file inspection.
objections.evidence: commands/dev.md:134-136 contains agent enumeration list; grep returns no 'test-writer' there. BA's Step-by-step search pattern (P1.1, ticket line 55) did not include commands/dev.md as a target for the agent-list search.
objections.proposed_fix: Extend AC-02 check.checks[] to include a third entry: {file: 'commands/dev.md', regex: 'test-writer', must_match: true} scoped near line 134-136. Update ticket M-P1.1b to mention this third site. Dev must add 'test-writer' to the fallback enumeration loop.

objections.finding: AC-03 must_not_contain_regex 'Write\s+tests/generated/manifest\.json(?!\s*\(global)' does not match the actual offending text in agents/test-writer.md. The current procedure step 5 line 183 reads literally 'Compute the new manifest. Write `tests/generated/manifest.json`.' — the path is BACKTICKED. The regex 'Write\s+tests/generated/manifest\.json' would not match because the literal text has 'Write `tests/generated/manifest.json`' with a backtick between 'Write' and the path. Dev could leave the offending line untouched and AC-03 would PASS_AS_SUBSTITUTE because the must_not_contain regex never fires. Also: the must_contain '{kind:"index"' literal at JSON line 86 enforces a non-JSON-ish bracket form that current line 119 satisfies as '"kind": "index"' — different spacing → either AC over-specifies or under-specifies.
objections.evidence: agents/test-writer.md:183 reads `5. Compute the new manifest. Write \`tests/generated/manifest.json\`.` — backticks around path. agents/test-writer.md:198 reads `"manifest_path": "tests/generated/manifest.json",` — different shape. agents/test-writer.md:119 reads `"kind": "index",` (with quotes and space). AC-03 check.must_contain expects `{kind:"index"` (no space, no leading quote on 'kind') — does not match real JSON.
objections.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC-03 check to: (a) must_not_contain_regex should account for backtick wrap: `Write\s+\`?tests/generated/manifest\.json\`?(?!\s*\(global)`; (b) must_contain should target the actual per-task path string `tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json` (already in JSON line 85) AND require the report.manifest_path field to NOT equal the global path; (c) drop the {kind:"index" requirement or rewrite as `"kind":\s*"index"` regex to match real JSON formatting. Scope the must_not_contain to the Procedure step 5 paragraph + Output JSON block only, not file-wide.

objections.finding: AC-04 must_not_contain forbids the literal phrase 'Read `tests/generated/manifest.json`. For every `active_tests[]` entry' — which correctly catches the Cycle-1-drift at qa.md:1000. But the check also forces dev to RIP OUT the line entirely. Meanwhile qa.md:1004 has a LEGITIMATE sentinel `if test_writer_expected = true AND tests/generated/manifest.json is missing → critical finding` — this checks PRESENCE of the global index file (which under Option B test-writer DOES still write as the {kind:index, tasks[]} index). The fix is to UPDATE the path references to per-task in lines 1000-1004 AND update schema lines 1434-1435, while preserving the global-presence sentinel as a legitimate cross-check. AC-04 as written does not distinguish, so dev may delete the legitimate sentinel.
objections.evidence: agents/qa.md:1000 (drift to fix: read active_tests from global file), qa.md:1004 (legitimate sentinel: check global-index presence), qa.md:1434-1435 (schema field manifest_path = global — should become per-task). AC-04 must_not_contain has only one phrase and must_contain has only the per-task path; no provision for preserving the global-presence check.
objections.proposed_fix: Split AC-04 into two checks: (a) Phase 5 step 1 MUST read per-task manifest (not global) — update must_not_contain to scope-limited regex; (b) the global-index PRESENCE sentinel at line 1004 stays as-is and is REQUIRED to remain (must_contain 'tests/generated/manifest.json is missing' or equivalent). Schema field manifest_path MUST be per-task path (must_contain_regex '"manifest_path":\s*"tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest\.json"').

objections.finding: AC-05 must_not_contain_regex 'tests/generated/manifest\.json(?!\s*\(index)' applies FILE-WIDE to commands/dev.md. Current state has THREE sites (lines 633, 637, 666) where the global path appears. Under Option B (per-task active + global index), the test-writer must STILL write the global index — so at least one mention of `tests/generated/manifest.json` legitimately remains (e.g., as 'Manifest: tests/generated/manifest.json (index)' at the test-writer dispatch input). The literal `(index)` annotation is unusual prose; dev may write 'global index manifest at tests/generated/manifest.json' or 'index file: tests/generated/manifest.json' which BOTH FAIL the negative lookahead. AC-05 forces an artificial annotation token rather than verifying semantic correctness. Per QA Counter-Evidence Authority Dimension 5 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift), this requires BA to update the AC to a runnable form.
objections.evidence: commands/dev.md:633 ('Manifest: tests/generated/manifest.json'), :637 ('verify on disk that `tests/generated/manifest.json` AND ... BOTH exist'), :666 ('Generated tests (when test-writer ran): tests/generated/<task_id>/ + tests/generated/manifest.json'). Of these, line 633 is the dispatch INPUT to test-writer (needs per-task path AND tells test-writer where global index is); line 637 is the post-dispatch existence check (can verify per-task OR both); line 666 is the handoff to Dev (needs per-task path).
objections.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC-05 as two checks: (a) Step 11 QA-dispatch (or the specific manifest-path-passed-to-QA line) MUST contain 'tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json' — scope to QA dispatch block only by line-range or by anchoring on adjacent 'manifest_path' keyword; (b) drop the file-wide must_not_contain — instead require that the dev dispatch handoff at line 666 also use the per-task path. Allow global-index references to coexist where semantically correct.

objections.finding: AC-07 requires `git ls-files agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` to return empty. This is correct DERIVED work (the gitignore-append is a no-op for already-tracked files), but Section 7 of the spec literally says only 'Append to .gitignore'. BA's risk paragraph (ticket line 161) describes git rm --cached as removing 47 files including ACTIVE cp-state files for THIS CYCLE. The risk paragraph claims 're-created by next spec invocation' but cp-state-ba-12, cp-state-qa-7 etc. are CURRENTLY IN USE — dev running `git rm --cached .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` mid-cycle removes the index entries for files that this very cycle is reading/writing. As codex notes: --cached preserves working-tree copies so enforcement does not break, but BA's authorization-chain claim should be explicit that this is derived cleanup, not literal Section-7 work.
objections.evidence: Section 7 ticket lines 359-362 say 'Append to .gitignore'; no mention of git rm. .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-ba-12.json and cp-state-qa-7.json ARE actively used by this cycle. Working-tree is preserved by --cached (verified by Bash hook logic).
objections.proposed_fix: BA must clarify in the ticket that AC-07 is DERIVED work (necessary side-effect of M-P2.3, not a separate user-stated requirement). Add explicit constraint: dev MUST use `git rm --cached` (never `git rm` or `git rm -r` without --cached). Add explicit forbidden-action: dev MUST NOT delete working-tree cp-state files. The orchestrator should pre-authorize this explicitly rather than BA inferring 'user implicit authorization'. Recommend: classify under out_of_scope_observations or escalate to user-confirmation before dev acts.

objections.finding: AC-09 check.checks[].must_contain_any = ['agent-scores.json', '.claude/specs'] is trivially satisfied. A stray python comment containing either string passes the check, even if the dev never adds the literal to the SKIP_FILES set or IGNORE_DIRS literal. Also `.lock` is not required — dev could add agent-scores.json to SKIP_FILES but forget agent-scores.json.lock and AC-09 would still pass. Per QA Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (never substitute related-but-different verification), this is a PASS_AS_SUBSTITUTE drift.
objections.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:215-230 uses must_contain_any with two loose strings; no requirement that the strings be inside the SKIP_FILES literal AST or that .lock be present.
objections.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC-09 with structural checks: (a) hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py SKIP_NAMES literal MUST be a Python set containing both 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock' (parse the AST or use a tighter regex anchored to `SKIP_NAMES\s*=\s*\{[^}]*'agent-scores\.json'[^}]*'agent-scores\.json\.lock'`); (b) hooks/doc_sync/tree.py SKIP_FILES + IGNORE_DIRS similarly; (c) optionally add a runtime test: invoke regen_readme.py with a fixture containing these files and assert they are excluded from output.

objections.finding: AC-10 check.checks[] uses must_count_gte (1, 1, 3) on raw venv/bin/activate string count per file. This does NOT verify PRECEDENCE — dev could add three `# source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate` comments in a header and pass the check while every python3 invocation still runs outside the venv. Per QA Anti-Fraud Principle 5 (related-but-different verification), this is PASS_AS_SUBSTITUTE.
objections.evidence: acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:237-260 uses raw regex count; ticket-20260520-221452.md:235 prose says 'each such line MUST be inside an execution context where source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate has already executed' — the prose is precedence-based but the JSON check is count-based.
objections.proposed_fix: Replace AC-10 with per-invocation precedence verification: for each non-comment line containing 'python3' in the file, check that one of: (a) the same line contains 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&' before 'python3' (subshell form like `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - ... )`); (b) a preceding line in the same function body (between the opening `function name() {` and the python3 line) contains 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate' without intervening function-boundary tokens. Implement as a small parser or a per-file regex with surrounding context. Alternatively: bind dev to use ONLY the subshell-wrap form so the check is a simple inline-regex `\(\s*source\s+[^)]*venv/bin/activate\s*&&\s*python3`.

objections.finding: AC-11 ticket prose (line 237-241) requires both label elimination AND cross-reference rewrites at former lines 844 and 1115 to integer-step prose. AC-11 JSON check only enforces `must_not_contain_regex: Sub-step 12\.\d` — this catches label elimination but not cross-reference rewrites. Dev could remove the two `Sub-step 12.x` labels and leave the cross-references as `proceed to the decision tree.` or `Summarise score-update events` without specifying that the prose links back to the new integer-step form. AC fails to enforce the prose requirement.
objections.evidence: ticket-20260520-221452.md:241 mandates 'cross-references at the former lines 844 and 1115 MUST be rewritten to refer to integer-step prose'; acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json:265-275 has only must_not_contain_regex check, no cross-reference verification.
objections.proposed_fix: Add a positive check to AC-11: must_contain_regex anchored to the former cross-reference locations — e.g., near line 844 must contain a phrase referring to the new integer-step form like 'Step 12 spec section updates' (or 'After applying spec section updates,'); near line 1115 must contain something like 'Step 12 score-update events' or 'score-update events from Step 12'. The exact phrasing is dev's choice but the AC should verify some integer-anchored prose exists at those locations.

objections.finding: AC-13 check.must_contain = ['spec-20260518-225715', 'rank', 'AskUserQuestion'] + check.must_contain_regex = '(comments|diagnostic).*English' — all unscoped, all file-wide. The strings 'rank' and 'AskUserQuestion' could appear ANYWHERE in style-inspector.md (existing or new content) and the check passes. The (comments|diagnostic).*English regex is already partially satisfied by current line 320 which contains 'Code/script comments and user-visible diagnostic strings (BLOCKED stderr, REASON lines, error messages) must be English'. Dev could add a single sentence 'spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 rank labels and AskUserQuestion choices' anywhere in the file and trivially pass — without writing the required structural exemption paragraph after the existing Exemption block. Per QA Anti-Fraud Principle 5 + Dimension 5 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift), this is PASS_AS_SUBSTITUTE drift.
objections.evidence: agents/style-inspector.md:320 already reads 'Code/script comments and user-visible diagnostic strings ... must be English' — partially satisfies regex. AC-13 has no scope to Standard 6 section, no positional requirement near line 320.
objections.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC-13 with structural scope: (a) extract the Standard 6 section as text between '### Standard 6: English Only' and the next '### Standard 7' heading (or equivalent boundary); (b) within that scoped text, require a NEW paragraph (in addition to existing 'Exemption (verbatim user-binding quotes)' at line 320) that contains ALL three of: (i) literal `spec-20260518-225715 §5.1`, (ii) at least one Chinese-character rank label sample (e.g., one of `见习学徒|初级工匠|熟练工匠|资深工匠|宗师级`), (iii) the literal phrase `AskUserQuestion`, AND a re-affirmation that 'comments' AND 'diagnostic' (or stderr) remain English-only. The check should use Python regex with text-slicing rather than file-wide must_contain. Alternatively: dev writes the exemption with a stable anchor like `**Exemption (Cycle-2 user-facing Chinese strings)**` and AC-13 verifies that anchor exists with the required attribution + enumeration.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: BA risk paragraph asserts 'agent-scores.json is per-machine runtime state, not user-authored content; cp-state files are per-cycle harness telemetry. Both belong in .gitignore from inception and the original tracking was unintended leakage.' This justification is reasonable but the user has NOT directly authorized git rm --cached in this session — BA inferred authorization. Orchestrator should escalate the git rm --cached step to user-confirmation before dev acts on it (especially the .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/ removal, which removes active cp-state index entries for this very cycle).

47 cp-state and lock files currently tracked under this dir. After `git rm --cached`, the working-tree copies survive (verified by --cached semantics) so the current cycle's spec-check.py read/write loop is NOT broken. However, future cycles using --spec will start with no tracked cp-state index — first commit after this cycle will leave them untracked permanently (which is the intent of the .gitignore append). Verified safe but worth noting for orchestrator commit-phase awareness.

The dispatch prompt notes the blast-radius map is 6.1MB / 26555 edges — scope filter likely failed. Did not verify in detail but flag for BA/Dev awareness: blast-radius Phase 2 (QA-side) may produce spurious gap declarations if the map is unfiltered. Recommend Dev cite explicit blast_radius_declarations[] only for files actually in the cycle's files_modified[].

scope_alignment_summary.scope_expansion_assessment: BA's 5 codex-extension ACs are JUSTIFIED completeness fixes — each closes a no-op gap in a Section-7 item (e.g., gitignore-append is a no-op without git rm --cached for tracked files; tool-policy entry is a no-op if test-writer is not in CP_AGENTS). They are not P4 leakage. However, BA should label these explicitly as 'derived from Section 7 X' rather than as standalone scope additions.

ba_evidence_verified_pass: tool-policy roles count = 24, no test-writer entry (verified)
5 python3 sites in 3 score/canary scripts; 0 venv activations (verified)
commands/dev.md Sub-step 12.0 at 822, Sub-step 12.1 at 861, cross-refs at 844 and 1115 (verified)
style-inspector.md Standard 6 at line 310, existing exemption at line 320 (verified)
test-writer.md internal contradiction between procedure step 5 (line 183) and schema section (line 80-128) (verified)
Risk paragraph claim 'no tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json files exist' — verified (tests/generated/ does not exist)
ba_evidence_gaps: BA missed commands/dev.md:134-136 third agent-list site (codex finding #2 confirmed)
BA did not verify that AC-13 strings already partially appear in style-inspector.md line 320
BA did not test whether AC-03 regex matches the actual backticked text at test-writer.md:183

summary.verdict_rationale: 10 of 13 ACs require revision. The spec_text_vs_execution_drift count (6) is unusually high — most ACs were written with substring/count-based checks that can pass without forcing dev to actually do the work. BA must regenerate the acceptance-criteria JSON with structural, scope-anchored checks before dev cycle can proceed.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed 8 of QA's 10 candidate objections (AC-03, AC-04, AC-05, AC-07, AC-09, AC-10, AC-11, AC-13) and added 2 new ones (AC-01 tools-list mismatch; AC-02 missing third agent-list site in commands/dev.md:134-136). Codex's PROPOSED_FIXes were incorporated into the objection list. Codex did not push back on AC-07 as OBSERVATION_ONLY rather than a blocker — agreed and documented as scope_alignment minor + out_of_scope_observation.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 10 codex findings became objections OBJ-01..OBJ-10. AC-07 demoted to minor severity per codex's enforcement-not-broken observation. AC-13 elevated to critical per QA Anti-Fraud Principle 5 because the pass-as-substitute drift would silently land a non-fix.

critical_issues: AC-13 pass-as-substitute: dev could pass without writing the structural exemption
AC-01 tools-list mismatch: AC would pass with a 3-tool list that leaves test-writer non-functional
refined_context.recommended_approach: BA must regenerate acceptance-criteria-20260520-221452.json with the per-AC PROPOSED_FIX content from each objection. Key pattern: replace must_contain_any / file-wide must_contain with scope-anchored regex (extract section, then check within section). Replace count-based checks (must_count_gte) with precedence-based checks (per-invocation precedence verification). Ensure ticket prose requirements (tools list, cross-reference rewrites) actually map to JSON check structure.
refined_context.additional_context: BA's investigation evidence is largely accurate — all factual claims about current file state verified pass. The defect is at the AC-encoding layer: BA correctly identified WHAT must change but wrote checks too loose to enforce it. Out-of-scope observations: orchestrator should escalate git rm --cached step to explicit user confirmation; blast-radius map is 6.1MB (likely unfiltered).
